taskDescription: Click on the key physiological terms in the text below.
textField:
The cell membrane is a *phospholipid* *bilayer* that controls the movement of substances into and out of the cell. *Integral* proteins span the entire membrane and may function as channels or carriers, while *peripheral* proteins are attached to the surface and often serve as enzymes or structural anchors. The *fluid* *mosaic* model describes the membrane as a dynamic, flexible structure.

The *resting* *membrane* *potential* of a neuron is maintained by the *sodium-potassium* *ATPase* pump and selective ion permeability. *Depolarisation* occurs when sodium channels open, allowing Na+ to rush into the cell. *Repolarisation* restores the negative resting potential through potassium efflux. The *refractory* period prevents immediate re-excitation of the membrane.

Body fluids are divided into *intracellular* and *extracellular* compartments. The extracellular fluid includes *interstitial* fluid surrounding cells and *plasma* within blood vessels. *Osmolarity* is the measure of solute concentration per litre of solution and is critical for maintaining fluid balance across compartments.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the key terminology of general physiology.
Good effort! Some important terms were missed.
Excellent! You identified the key physiological terms.
